feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Community
feed.subtitle: SAP Community blog posts in SAP Community

entry.title: SAP Community CPH Event Plan - 2026
entry.content:
SAP Community Copenhagen – Event Plan for 2026

We are excited to share our SAP Community plan for 2026!

This year we are aiming at organizing at least four events – one per quarter – combining professional inspiration with cozy community social time.

In Q1, before Easter, we will do a social meet-up. This will be a great opportunity to reconnect, meet new members, and strengthen our community. The date will be the 26th of March and the event for sign up will be shared shortly.

In Q2, at the end of May after the SAPPHIRE conference in Madrid (19-21 May), we will host an event where we would like to invite 1–2 speakers from Denmark or abroad to share key announcements and highlights from SAPPHIRE. We are aiming at the 28th of May for this event.

After the summer holidays, in Q3, we will meet again for a social event while the weather is still good – a perfect setting for socializing and sharing our holiday pictures.

Finally, after SAP TechEd in the fourth quarter, we are planning a half-day or full-day event with inspirational talks from speakers from Denmark and abroad, focusing on the latest trends and insights. 

The dates for the second half of 2026 are still TBD. 

On behalf of the SAP Community CPH organizers, we are really looking forward to an inspiring year together and to growing our SAP Community even stronger in 2026!

Juan, Prakash, Pallavi, Christian, Asier

entry.title: SAP Community Brasil 2026!!!! Muitos eventos rolando pelo País e você vai ficar de fora?
entry.content:
Olá Sapistas,

O carnaval terminou e  agora é hora de começar o ano de 2026.

A nossa comunidade estará fervendo nesse ano com a quantidade de eventos que teremos pelo nosso País. 

Não perca a oportunidade de fazer parte desse networking e a aprimorar o seu conhecimento.

Espero encontrar com vocês nos diversos eventos pelo Brasil. 😍

Este blog será atualizado à cada evento. Por isso, deixe aqui a sua mensagem, a sua foto. 

Compartilhe  e venha para a SAP Community 

Abraços.

 @RobertaFernande   e   @antonia_zorn 

 

02/03LinkCampinas Build AI services using the CAP and Generative AI Hub CodeJam
03/03LinkSão Paulo Joule Studio: Create Joule Skills and Agents Without Coding CodeJam
03/03LinkSão Paulo São Paulo meetup

05/03LinkRio de Janeiro Getting started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core CodeJam

10/04LinkCuritiba Getting started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core CodeJam

entry.title: SAP Stammtisch Plus - Pune (March 2026)
entry.content:
Greetings SAP Community in Pune! We have some exciting news to share with you!


SAP Community events are back in Pune!

We are thrilled to announce that we’re launching a series of quarterly SAP Stammtisch Plus gatherings, beginning with our first meetup on March 14th, 2026.

This event is an excellent opportunity for SAP professionals, enthusiasts, and anyone interested in learning more about SAP technologies to come together, learn, network with like-minded individuals, and have fun.

Are you wondering what an SAP Stammtisch Plus event is?

SAP Stammtisch Plus is a local grassroots community event where SAP Community members come together to share knowledge about SAP-related topics. The event's primary focus is networking & discussions, with 2 or 3 sessions from community members.

Learn more about it at: SAP Stammtisch - what is it about?

What is in it for you?

Let’s cut to the chase, what will you get by attending or contributing to this event?

1. Networking – You get to meet like-minded tech folks and have more space for networking.
2. Learning – You will learn about the latest SAP technologies from experts.

Theme

Decoding Business AI: From Embedded Intelligence to Enterprise Impact

When?
March 14th, 2026, from 9:30 AM till 12:30 PM. Registration starts at 9:00 AM.

Where?
PRIMUS HQ, Survey No 34/3, Plot No 4,5,6, Rohan Seher Lane,
Pan Card Club Rd, Baner, Pune, Maharashtra - 411045

Registration

Please register using this link: https://events.primustechsys.com/stammtisch2026 

Note: We have only limited seats available, so register ONLY if you plan to attend IN-PERSON on March 14, Saturday in Pune.

Organizers & Volunteers:

  • @Parineeta_Somvanshi 
  • @Punith_Oswal 
  • @Asutosh21 


We hope to see you all there and look forward to an engaging and productive event. Don't forget to register using the link provided above to secure your spot. See you soon!

Best Regards,
SAP Community Group – Pune

entry.title: Sydney Stammtisch Recap Feb '26 -
entry.content:
A great time was had by all 'chatting in The Chapel' on Thursday night.  Yet again we had a rainy evening and we were glad for the SAUG (user group) meeting because that kept our numbers up and we had around 35 attendees in total I would say.  We were also glad that The Greenwood had room for us inside The Chapel bar.

We lucky to have some out of town guests like Doug (Munford), @ChrisRae & Shaun Bradbridge

Thanks to everyone for coming, see you again around May time.......

Sydney Stammtisch #SAPStammtischSYD #SAP Community

entry.title: Серія вебінарів у березні
entry.content:
Березень в SAP буде максимально насиченим подіями для тих, хто будує хмарну стратегію, працює з даними та AI, відповідає за безпеку чи підтримку користувачів. Обирайте свої must‑have вебінари та реєструйтесь за лінками нижче.

Хмара, виробництво та операції

  • 2 березня, 15:00–16:00
    Accelerate to the Cloud: SAPME/MII/PCo to SAP Digital Manufacturing
    Практичні шляхи переходу до SAP Digital Manufacturing: можливості рішень, Customer Evolution Kit, варіанти міграції та реальні кейси, що допоможуть знизити ризики й скоротити time‑to‑value.

  • 10 березня, 15:00–16:00
    SAP Cloud Application Services for Best Practices Cloud Operations
    Як керовані сервіси CAS знімають з команди рутину підтримки хмарних ландшафтів, закриваючи питання продуктивності, безпеки та інтеграцій, щоб ви фокусувались на стратегії.

  • 17 березня, 15:00–16:00
    Move Travel & Expense to the Cloud with SAP Concur
    Вебінар про перенесення процесів відряджень і витрат у хмару з SAP Concur та побудову прозорого end‑to‑end‑процесу.

Дані, аналітика та SAP Business Data Cloud

  • 5 березня, 17:00–18:00
    From Data to Decisions: Analytics with SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP BDC
    Як поєднати SAP Analytics Cloud і SAP Business Data Cloud, щоб отримати єдине джерело правди для аналітики, планування та прогнозування без дублювання даних.

  • 12 березня, 17:00–18:00
    The Next Era of Business Data – From Data Silos to Business Insight: The Power of BDC Connect
    Zero‑copy підхід, інтеграція з Databricks і Snowflake, усунення data silos та побудова єдиного business data fabric для аналітики й AI.

  • 26 березня, 17:00–18:00
    The Next Era of Business Data – Design. Govern. Deliver. Data Products – Data Product Studio
    Огляд SAP Data Product Studio: моделювання, збагачення та управління data products на базі SAP Business Data Cloud з повним контролем семантики та продуктивності.

SAP BTP та AI

  • 10 березня, 17:00–18:00
    Architect the Future: SAP BTP Core Revealed
    Ключові можливості SAP Business Technology Platform: рантайми, безпека, ідентифікація, конективність та операції в гібридних і multi‑cloud сценаріях.

  • 24 березня, 15:00–16:00
    About Agents and Jewels: SAP Business AI
    Роль AI‑агентів та асистентів у стратегії SAP, практичні сценарії з SAP Business AI та взаємодія з партнерами для реалізації цінності AI в бізнес‑процесах.

  • 24 березня, 17:00–18:00
    Architect the Future: Turning Ideas into Enterprise Apps on SAP BTP
    Від коду до оркестрованих бізнес‑процесів: фреймворки, події, API та workflow‑оркестрація для швидкої розробки й інтеграції рішень на SAP BTP.

  • 31 березня, 15:00–16:00
    AI Commercial Discovery
    Огляд поточної комерційної моделі AI від SAP: безкоштовні та платні пропозиції, прогнозування споживання для підписок та кастомних агентів, моніторинг використання.

  • 14 квітня, 17:00–18:00
    Architect the Future: SAP BTP AI Best Practices – From Strategy to Implementation
    Best practices та шаблони для побудови промислових AI‑рішень на SAP BTP: як структурувати use cases, обрати підхід до реалізації та масштабувати AI з відчутним бізнес‑ефектом.

SAP Cloud ERP та оновлення продуктів

  • 12 березня, 12:00–12:30
    Continuous Innovation: Feb 2026 Updates for SAP Cloud ERP Private (MEE/EMEA)
    Огляд нового релізу SAP Cloud ERP Private та його впливу на весь ланцюг створення цінності.

  • 18 березня, 15:00–16:00
    Discover the latest Innovations in SAP Cloud ERP Private – FPS01 Release
    Інновації релізу 2025 FPS01: Joule та AI‑сценарії у продажах, логістиці, PLM, сервісі тощо, а також програми сервісів і підтримки для вашої cloud‑подорожі.

Підтримка, SAP for Me та взаємодія з SAP

  • 4 березня, 16:00–17:00
    CIC Support SAP for Me: SAP for Me Demo
    Огляд порталу SAP for Me та функції Get Support – як швидко знаходити потрібну інформацію й звертатися до підтримки.

  • 12 березня, 11:30–12:30
    CIC Support SAP for Me: Case Management
    Покрокова робота з Get Support, створення якісних кейсів і процеси SAP, яких варто дотримуватись для ефективної взаємодії з підтримкою.

  • 16 березня, 16:30–17:30
    CIC Support SAP for Me: Getting the Most from your Support
    Інфраструктура SAP Support, управління користувачами, завантаження ПЗ, ліцензії, системна адміністрація, сповіщення, сервісні запити та роль Customer Interaction Center.

  • 3 березня, 12:00–13:00
    Success Unleashed: Be future‑ready with SAP Services and Support (EMEA)
    Новий engagement‑підхід SAP Services & Support, який допоможе впевнено стартувати, зберігати імпульс змін та розкрити повну цінність інвестицій у SAP.

Безпека та відповідальне AI

  • 19 березня, 11:30–12:30 (EMEA)
    SAP Security Webcast in March: SAP’s Leadership in AI Governance with ISO 42001
    Історія отримання сертифікації ISO 42001, підходи SAP до AI‑governance на базі ISO/IEC 42001 та NIST AI RMF, вплив на сервіси Joule, SAP AI Core та SAP Business AI й підготовка до регуляцій на кшталт EU AI Act.

Стратегія даних та AI

  • 24 березня, 12:00–13:00
    The Fabric of Data & AI – Insights from leaders shaping the future of data and AI
    Дискусія з Irfan Khan та лідерами індустрії про те, як бізнес‑data fabric поєднує дані, метадані й процеси, щоб AI спирався на реальний бізнес‑контекст та приносив вимірювані результати.

Інтеграція

  • 31 березня, 17:00–18:00
    SAP Integration Suite – Monthly Update March
    Оновлення ключових можливостей SAP Integration Suite та інтерактивний блок #AskAnythingIntegration із експертами.

Щоб забронювати місце на вебінарах, переходьте за посиланнями та додавайте події до свого календаря – березень стане чудовою можливістю прокачати хмару, дані, AI та підтримку в екосистемі SAP.

entry.title: SAP Connect happening in Sydney on 17th March! Register Now!
entry.content:
Registration is free:  https://events.sap.com/au-sap-connnect-for-data-it-sydney/en_us/home.html#accordion-2ca945c052-item-5ee2c37dd3

Turn Complexity into Agility with Connected Data and AI

IT leaders are under pressure to drive innovation while increasing agility in an increasingly complex, AI-driven world. Join us to discover how technology leaders are connecting applications, data and AI to build intelligent, adaptive enterprises that thrive in constant change.

You’ll learn how to:

  • Harmonise SAP and non-SAP data to create a trusted foundation for AI

  • Unlock real-time insights by unifying data across systems

  • Automate core processes to drive agility and resilience

  • Modernise ERP with industry-specific, AI-enabled capabilities

  • Maximise the value of AI, data and applications through a business technology platform

Join us in Sydney on 17 March 2026 at Crown Sydney for a high-impact day of keynotes, customer stories and solution deep dives designed for CIOs, CTOs, architects and digital leaders looking to turn complexity into competitive advantage.

entry.title: Emerging Technology and Caged Thoughts
entry.content:
The boom that  we are witnessing now is louder than anything our ancestors could have  ever imagined and faster than any academician could ever track.Technology no longer evolves year by year,it now evolves month by month, week by week and now hour by hour. What once took decades now takes days. This is tremendous growth. Revolutionary. Historic.

But growth at this speed comes with a  psychological cost.

With every new model, every new breakthrough, a silent companion , imposter syndrome appears.A creeping anxiety that whispers: “You are far behind.”

As a master’s student, I constantly feel the pressure to keep up with emerging technologies. The journey no longer moves from one programming language to another. It leaps from machine learning to deep learning, from ChatGPT to Gemini, Claude, AI agents, Agentic AI and beyond.

By the time I begin understanding one trend, another replaces it.This endless tracking doesn’t always create productivity  but hinders it and comes along procrastination. A procrastination phase so deep it feels like sinking into the Mariana Trench, surrounded by information, yet unable to move.

In 2026, I thought I had found clarity: “I need a Vision Board.” Then another dilemma arises.Should I use Pinterest? A vision board app? A productivity tool? A Notion template?Humorous, that even organizing clarity now requires choosing between technologies.And that’s when I realized something important:-

The problem isn’t the growth of technology, the problem is trying to grow in every direction at once.

“If you are planning to dwell in tech, delve deeper first, then broader.”

Depth builds confidence,breadth without depth builds anxiety.Emerging technology is expanding at a humongous pace , at the same time our thoughts don’t need to be caged by it.We don’t need to learn everything, sometimes, the most revolutionary act in a fast-moving world
is choosing where to stand still.

entry.title: Hello SAP Community Chisinau!
entry.content:
This group aims to be a platform for exchange among various stakeholders in the SAP ecosystem. Whether you work directly for SAP, deal with SAP solutions in other companies, or are employed as a freelance or in-house SAP consultant – you're in the right place. This group is also for those who have had little or no direct contact with SAP but are curious about it, such as students, trainees, educators, or anyone generally interested in innovation and technology.

A special focus of our Chisinau group is to establish connections within the local SAP ecosystem and the growing tech and innovation community in Moldova. We also want to create links to the country’s universities, educational institutions, and research organizations. Our goal is to connect as many interested individuals as possible and foster knowledge sharing and collaboration.

To support this exchange, we plan to host regular local events in the future. We will kick things off with a new SAP Stammtisch in Chisinau (details will follow in a separate post). Additional event formats are already in preparation and will offer a diverse and engaging program. Stay tuned!

The group thrives on your participation and engagement. Therefore, it's open to everyone who identifies with the goals and principles of this group. We're always looking for fresh ideas and dedicated minds. If you'd like to get actively involved, additional group owners or curators (up to 6) are very welcome.

We look forward to a vibrant and exciting community in Chisinau!

Best regards

Natalia

Foto: Dorin Seremet/Unsplash 

 

Salut, SAP Community Chișinău!

Suntem încântați să vă urăm bun venit la lansarea noii SAP Community Chișinău!

Acest grup își propune să fie o platformă de schimb de idei și experiență între diverși actori din ecosistemul SAP. Indiferent dacă lucrați direct pentru SAP, utilizați soluții SAP în alte companii sau activați ca consultant SAP, fie freelance, fie intern într-o organizație – sunteți în locul potrivit. Grupul este deschis și pentru cei care au avut puțin sau deloc contact direct cu SAP, dar sunt curioși să afle mai multe, **bleep** ar fi studenți, stagiari, educatori sau orice persoană interesată de inovație și tehnologie.

Un obiectiv important al grupului nostru din Chișinău este să dezvolte conexiuni în cadrul ecosistemului local SAP și al comunității în creștere de tehnologie și inovație din Moldova. De asemenea, dorim să construim legături cu universitățile, instituțiile educaționale și organizațiile de cercetare din țară. Scopul nostru este să conectăm cât mai multe persoane interesate și să încurajăm schimbul de cunoștințe și colaborarea.

Pentru a sprijini acest schimb, planificăm să organizăm în viitor evenimente locale regulate. Vom începe cu un SAP Meetup în Chișinău (detaliile vor urma într-o postare separată). Alte formate de evenimente sunt deja în pregătire și vor oferi un program divers și interesant. Rămâneți aproape!

Acest grup se bazează pe participarea și implicarea voastră. De aceea, este deschis tuturor celor care se regăsesc în obiectivele și principiile acestei comunități. Suntem mereu în căutare de idei noi și de oameni dedicați. Dacă doriți să vă implicați activ, noi proprietari sau curatori ai grupului (până la 6) sunt bineveniți.

Așteptăm cu nerăbdare să construim o comunitate SAP vibrantă și dinamică în Chișinău!

Cu drag,
Natalia

Foto: Dorin Seremet/Unsplash

entry.title: ABAP Cloud Learning Map 2026
entry.content:
Pessoal,

SAP Liberou o Mapa de aprendizagem do ABAP Cloud,  ele oferece um bom ponto de partida para começar a conhecer as tecnologias, ferramentas, abordagens e características do ABAP Cloud, além de obter informações novas ou que estejam faltando, com base no seu próprio nível de conhecimento

Segue o link :

https://dam.sap.com/mac/app/p/pdf/asset/preview/7YM5vMP?ltr=a&rc=10&doi=SAP1280868

Muito interessante!

Aproveitem!

SAP S/4HANA Cloud ABAP Environment

entry.title: Як прискорити перехід на SAP S/4HANA? Практичний інтенсив по Integrated Toolchain 🚀
entry.content:
Трансформація системи — це не лише технічне завдання, а стратегічний крок для бізнесу. Щоб зробити цей процес прозорим та керованим, SAP пропонує набір інтегрованих інструментів.

Запрошуємо на Hands-on Bootcamp: Leveraging the Integrated Toolchain for System Conversion! 🛠️

Про що ви дізнаєтесь? Ця сесія в реальному часі фокусується на тому, як поєднати методологію та інструментарій для успішної конверсії системи. Ми розберемо роль ключових компонентів:

🔹 SAP Signavio — для аналізу та оптимізації бізнес-процесів.

🔹 SAP LeanIX — для керування архітектурою та прозорості ІТ-ландшафту.

🔹 SAP Cloud ALM — для управління життєвим циклом рішення та координації впровадження.

Чому варто відвідати?

✅ Hands-on досвід: це не просто теорія, а практичні вправи.

✅ Наскрізний сценарій: ви побачите, як інструменти взаємодіють між собою на кожному етапі проєкту.

✅ Clean Core: дізнаєтесь, як підтримувати "чисте ядро" під час трансформації.

🔗Реєструйтеся за посиланням!

entry.title: 🎙️ Série da comunidade: Somos SAP Community Curitiba
entry.content:
Comunidade não é só evento.
É troca, conexão e pessoas que constroem juntas.


Pensando nisso, iniciamos a série Somos SAP Community Curitiba, um espaço para dar voz às pessoas que ajudam a fortalecer o ecossistema SAP na nossa cidade.


Mais do que falar sobre tecnologia, queremos compartilhar histórias reais, trajetórias, desafios e conexões que fazem a comunidade acontecer.
💛Valorizar quem constrói a comunidade
🔗Fortalecer conexões entre profissionais SAP
🚀Inspirar novas pessoas a entrarem no ecossistema
🌱Mostrar que comunidade é colaboração, não competição
👩‍💻Dar visibilidade a talentos locais


📌Este é o post de referência da série.

Aqui você encontra o template para quem for convidado(a) a participar.

📌COMO PARTICIPAR DA SÉRIE
Se você for convidado(a), siga este formato no seu post:


1️⃣Título do post
Comece seu post com o título:
Somos SAP Community Curitiba – [Seu nome]


2️⃣Comece citando este post de referência
Inclua o link deste post no início do seu texto.
Exemplo:
Esta publicação faz parte da série Somos SAP Community Curitiba.
O template da série está neste post de referência: [link deste post]


3️⃣Mencione quem te convidou
Comece dizendo quem te convidou para a série e mencione o post da pessoa.
Exemplo:
Fui convidado(a) por [@nome da pessoa] para participar da série
Somos SAP Community Curitiba.
Você pode conferir o post dela/dele aqui: [link do post]


4️⃣Responda às 3 perguntas

  • Quem sou eu na SAP Community Curitiba?
  • O que mais me motiva a continuar fazendo parte da SAP Community Curitiba?
  • Como fazer parte da comunidade impactou minha jornada profissional ou pessoal?


5️⃣Continue a corrente
Convide no mínimo 1 pessoa da comunidade para continuar a série.
Se quiser, você pode convidar mais pessoas também.
Exemplo:
Passando a corrente 🚀
Agora eu convido [@nome da pessoa] a contar um pouco da sua história e da sua conexão com a SAP Community Curitiba.


Minha pergunta para você é:
[Pergunta livre]


6️⃣Opcional
Você também pode incluir no seu post:
📷Uma foto
🔗Suas redes sociais


💡Dicas

  • Ao convidar alguém, lembre-se de avisar a pessoa para que ela saiba que foi convidada a continuar a série.
  • Fique à vontade para compartilhar o link do seu post também no LinkedIn, usando as hashtags da série para ajudar a ampliar a visibilidade da comunidade.

 

Hashtags da série
#SomosSAPCommunityCWB
#SAPCommunityCWB
#SAPCommunity


Se você faz parte da SAP Community Curitiba, essa corrente também é sua.

Vamos construir essa história juntos.


🚀Em breve publicarei o primeiro post iniciando essa série.


Comunidade se constrói com pessoas.
E agora queremos ouvir as histórias de quem faz a SAP Community Curitiba acontecer.
Se você faz parte da comunidade e gostaria de participar da série, deixe seu nome nos comentários deste post.
Será um prazer ampliar ainda mais essa corrente.

🔗Conecte-se com a SAP Community Curitiba
Quer acompanhar as novidades da comunidade ou participar das próximas iniciativas?
Siga nossos canais:
💼LinkedIn
📸Instagram
💬Grupo WhatsApp da comunidade


Assim você fica por dentro de eventos, encontros, conteúdos e iniciativas da SAP Community Curitiba.

entry.title: 📅 Save the Date — SAP Inside Track Belgium 2026
entry.content:
🏠Returning to where it all started

Hello SAP Community! 👋

We are thrilled to announce that SAP Inside Track Belgium is back in 2026 — and this time, we're going back to our roots. 🎉

We're heading back to Kontich, to the offices of Flexso at Prins Boudewijnlaan 24A — a place that holds a very special place in the history of #sitBE. There were editions here before my time organized by others, but for me personally, this is where it all started. Now we're writing the next chapter — together.

📋Event Details

🗓️ Date: Saturday, September 19th, 2026

📍Location: Flexso — Prins Boudewijnlaan 24A, 2550 Kontich, Belgium 🇧🇪

🌐Website: https://sitbe.fiddle.be

🤔What is SAP Inside Track?

An SAP Inside Track is a free, community-driven event open to everyone — customers, consultants, freelancers, developers, architects, and business professionals alike. The event is organized by peers, for peers. Sessions are driven by knowledge sharing, not sales pitches. Come to learn, share, connect, and be inspired.

🏢Hosted by Flexso

A heartfelt thank you to Flexso for opening their doors and welcoming the SAP community back. Flexso is an expert SAP partner and a strong supporter of the SAP community in Belgium, located right next to the E19 in Kontich.

🌐https://www.flexso.com/en/flexso-kontich

🙋 Get Involved

🎤Speak at #sitBE — A Call for Speakers will open soon. 20-min lightning talks and 40-min deep dives are both welcome!

🎫Register to attend — Spots are limited. Registration details coming soon — stay tuned!

📢Spread the word — Share this post and tag your SAP colleagues. The more the merrier!

We can't wait to see you in Kontich on September 19th. Mark your calendars!

entry.title: Somos SAP Community Curitiba – Simone Azevedo
entry.content:
Este post faz parte da série “Somos SAP Community Curitiba”, criada para compartilhar as histórias das pessoas que ajudam a construir e fortalecer o ecossistema SAP na nossa cidade.


A ideia da série foi apresentada neste post de abertura:
➡️Introdução - Série da comunidade: Somos SAP Community Curitiba

A proposta é simples: responder algumas perguntas e contar um pouco da nossa jornada dentro da comunidade.


👩‍💻Quem sou eu na SAP Community Curitiba?
Sou Simone Azevedo, consultora SAP e curadora da SAP Community Curitiba.
Meu papel na comunidade é ajudar a conectar pessoas, promover eventos e incentivar a troca de conhecimento entre profissionais do ecossistema SAP.
Acredito muito que comunidades são espaços poderosos para aprendizado, networking e crescimento profissional.


💙O que mais me motiva a continuar fazendo parte da SAP Community Curitiba?
O que mais me motiva são as pessoas.
Cada evento, cada encontro e cada conversa mostram que quando profissionais se reúnem para compartilhar conhecimento, todos crescem juntos.
Ver alguém sair inspirado de um evento, criar novas conexões ou descobrir novos caminhos na carreira é algo que realmente faz valer a pena.


🚀Como fazer parte da comunidade impactou minha jornada profissional ou pessoal?
A comunidade ampliou muito minha visão sobre o ecossistema SAP.
Além do crescimento profissional, ela me proporcionou algo ainda mais especial: amizades, parcerias e um verdadeiro senso de pertencimento.
A SAP Community Curitiba me ensinou que tecnologia conecta sistemas — mas comunidades conectam pessoas.


📸Um momento especial na comunidade


SAP Inside Track 2019 - 1ª Edição em Curitiba
Essa foto registra um momento muito especial da minha jornada na comunidade: a organização do primeiro SAP Inside Track em Curitiba.  Na foto, um time incrível de voluntários que ajudou a transformar esse evento em realidade. Muito obrigada! 💙
Meu agradecimento também ao @faustomotter (segunda foto) , pelo apoio e incentivo desde o início, e ao @Marssel700  , que mesmo atuando remotamente foi parte importante dessa construção.


🌐Conecte-se comigo
LinkedIn:
👉https://www.linkedin.com/in/simone-azevedo⁠


✨Convidados para continuar a série
Para continuar a série Somos SAP Community Curitiba, convido: @LuizSouza , @AdrianoCWB , @Marssel700 ,  @Raquelamar , @Fabiele , @ElisangelaMartins  , @lucaspletsch1 , @TalisMoraes , @Pr1sc1la , @thamires_k 
para compartilharem suas histórias e experiências na comunidade.


Além das perguntas da série:
1️⃣Quem sou eu na SAP Community Curitiba?
2️⃣O que mais me motiva a continuar fazendo parte da SAP Community Curitiba?
3️⃣Como fazer parte da comunidade impactou minha jornada profissional ou pessoal?


Deixo também uma pergunta adicional:
❓Que conselho você daria para alguém que está começando agora no ecossistema SAP e ainda não conhece a comunidade?


✨Frase final
A SAP Community Curitiba é feita por pessoas que acreditam no poder de compartilhar conhecimento, conectar profissionais e construir oportunidades juntos.
Cada história compartilhada fortalece ainda mais essa comunidade que tanto nos inspira.

#SomosSAPCommunityCWB
#SAPCommunityCWB
#SAPCommunity

entry.title: My SAP Business AI Certification Journey
entry.content:
When I started these SAP Business AI courses (Discovering SAP Business AI, Positioning SAP Business AI and Boosting AI-driven Business Transformation with Joule Agents), I expected a standard product overview. What I got instead was a completely new way of thinking about enterprise technology, AI strategy, and how to have meaningful conversations with clients about digital transformation.

This blog is my attempt to share what I learned, how I passed my certification, and what surprised me along the way — for anyone about to take the same journey.

SAP Certification Name: SAP Certified - Positioning SAP Business AI Solutions as part of SAP Business Suite (C_BCBAI)

1. What the Certification Covers

The certification spans three SAP Learning courses, each building on the previous one. Together they cover the full SAP Business AI story — from strategy to technical architecture to real-world deployment.

Course

Focus

Key Learning

Discovering SAP Business AI

Why AI? What is SAP's strategy?

The SAP Flywheel — how Applications, Data and AI reinforce each other

Positioning SAP Business AI

How to position AI to different personas

Joule, AI Foundation, Responsible AI, Sustainability — and how each lands with CIO, CFO, COO

Joule Agents & Transformation

How AI agents actually work in business

Agentic AI, Business Data Cloud, Knowledge Graph, AI Agent Hub — the full technical picture

2. The Three Things That Changed How I Think

A. AI Without Data Is Just Noise

The biggest insight from Course 1 was simple but profound: AI is only as good as the data feeding it. Before these courses, I used to talk about AI features. Now I start every conversation with the data foundation.

SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC) is what makes AI reliable — connecting all systems using zero-copy federation, preserving business context through the Semantic Layer, and keeping data sovereign in each region. Without BDC, AI outputs are guesswork.

B. Joule Is Not Just a Chatbot

Before the certification, I described Joule as "SAP's AI assistant." After it, I describe it very differently. Joule is an omnipresent AI Copilot with 1,800+ skills, covering 80% of SAP's most-used transactions, available in 11 languages, and capable of proactively acting before users even ask.

And then there are Joule Agents — a completely different level. Agents reason, plan, and execute multi-step workflows autonomously. The Cash Management Agent saves finance teams up to 70% of manual reconciliation time. The Production Planning Agent autonomously checks material and capacity. 

C. Responsible AI Is a Commercial Accelerator

I used to treat ethics and compliance as a defensive topic — something to address when clients raised concerns. The certification changed that. SAP's Responsible AI framework is actually a competitive advantage.

  • Client data never leaves SAP's governed architecture — never used to train third-party models
  • Every Joule action is fully auditable — no black-box decisions
  • Data sovereignty built in — GDPR in Europe, PDPA in Asia, automatic
  • ISO 42001 certified for AI governance — internationally recognised standard
  • Human-in-the-loop by design — AI recommends, humans approve critical decisions.

3. How I Applied the Learning in the Certification

The certification assessment is scenario-based — which I found genuinely challenging and genuinely useful. Here is what helped me prepare and what I would tell anyone taking it next.

Scenario Type

What It Tests

My Approach

Client objection handling

Can you position BDC and Joule against a "we don't need to change" mindset?

Memorised the control tower analogy — ERPs are engines, BDC is the control tower. Never replace, always layer.

Persona-based positioning

Which message lands with which persona — CIO vs CFO vs COO?

Built a mental matrix: CIO = integration + security, CFO = ROI + reconciliation time, COO = supply chain speed

Technical architecture

How does zero-copy federation work? What is the Knowledge Graph?

Used real examples: Knowledge Graph maps PO to Supplier to Invoice — grounding AI in business context, preventing hallucinations

Responsible AI scenarios

What happens to client data? How is compliance maintained?

Led with ISO 42001, data sovereignty, human-in-the-loop — not features, but governance principles

The exam rewards consultative thinking, not product recall. The question is rarely "what does Joule do?" It is almost always "given this client situation, what would you recommend and why?" Thinking like a consultant — not a salesperson — is the key.

4. What I Gained Beyond the Certificate

Certifications give you a credential. Good certifications give you a new lens. This one gave me both.

  • Confidence in client conversations — I can now discuss AI architecture, data sovereignty, and agent capabilities without hesitation or vagueness.
  • A structured positioning framework — I know which message to lead with for each persona and which technical detail to deploy for each objection.
  • Real examples that land — not "SAP has AI agents" but "the Cash Management Agent saves your finance team 70% of manual reconciliation time — here is exactly how."
  • A sustainability angle I was not expecting — the Green Ledger and Sustainability Tower capabilities genuinely surprised me with their depth and maturity.
  • A new appreciation for data — the courses reinforced that AI is a data story first and a technology story second. That reframing has changed how I approach every client engagement.
  • A structured positioning framework — I know which message to lead with for each persona and which technical detail to deploy for each objection.
  • Real examples that land — not "SAP has AI agents" but "the Cash Management Agent saves your finance team 70% of manual reconciliation time — here is exactly how."
  • A sustainability angle I was not expecting — the Green Ledger and Sustainability Tower capabilities genuinely surprised me with their depth and maturity.
  • A new appreciation for data — the courses reinforced that AI is a data story first and a technology story second. That reframing has changed how I approach every client engagement.

The most valuable moment in the entire certification was a scenario-based roleplay where I had to respond to a sceptical client who said "our ERP works fine, why change it?" Every concept from all three courses came together in that one moment. That is when I knew the learning had actually landed.

Final Thought

If you are considering this certification — do it. Not just for the badge, but for the way it reshapes how you think about AI, data, and enterprise transformation.

And when you sit down with your next client who says "we are curious about AI but not sure where to start" — you will know exactly what to say.

Start with the data. Layer the intelligence. Let the Flywheel turn.

If this was useful, please like and share. Questions or your own experiences? Drop them in the comments below. Happy to connect with fellow SAP learners.

entry.title: SAP Community Voice: Popular Blog Posts, ABAP Certification Updates, New Location Groups and More!
entry.content:
We're excited to bring you another edition filled with highlights and updates from across our global SAP Community.

From deep-dive technical blog posts on topics like Agentic AI and Cloud Integration alerts to the latest news around the ABAP Cloud certification and a recap of UI5con Bengaluru 2026. We're also happy to welcome three new SAP Community Location Groups: Chisinau, Linz, and Mexico! You will also find upcoming community events (including SAP Inside Track Madrid!) so you can stay engaged with what's happening across the SAP ecosystem.

Let's dive in!

Featured Blog Posts 

Agentic Reporting: AI-Driven Insights 

Most enterprise dashboards still focus on reporting what happened, leaving analysts to interpret whether KPI changes matter. In her recent blog post series, the SAP Community member Shruti Srivastava introduces the concept of Agentic Reporting, where AI-powered agents continuously observe data, understand context, and surface only the deviations that truly require attention. Framed within the SAP analytics landscape, including SAP S/4HANA, SAP Analytics Cloud, SAP Datasphere, and SAP Business Technology Platform, the post explains how agentic systems can move beyond rigid thresholds and static alerts by considering factors such as seasonality, historical patterns, and business context before raising an issue. Using a KPI breach monitoring example, it shows how the same metric change could either be ignored or escalated depending on contextual signals, with the agent providing root-cause insights and recommended actions. With clear comparisons and visuals illustrating the concept, we recommend reading for anyone interested in how AI and technologies like Joule could transform reporting from passive dashboards into proactive decision support. 

Read Shruti blog post here 

What's New in Asset Management in SAP Cloud ERP Private 2025 FPS01 

SAP Cloud ERP Private 2025 FPS01 introduces a wide range of enhancements across Asset Management, improving maintenance execution, safety compliance, planning, and technical object management. In this detailed blog post, the SAP expert Fernanda Rodrigues provides a curated overview of the most important innovations, including embedded Lockout/Tagout (LOTO) isolation management within permit-to-work processes, enhancements to maintenance orders and inspection checklists, improved handling of unplanned work confirmations, and usability upgrades in the Perform Maintenance Jobs app. The article also highlights planning improvements such as expanded maintenance plan capabilities, more flexible planning buckets, and better analytics for phase-based maintenance, along with master data innovations like unified technical object search and new reference equipment and structure management. With screenshots and practical explanations throughout, this post is a helpful read for maintenance technicians, planners, and reliability engineers who want to understand the latest capabilities and process improvements. 

Read Fernanda blog post here 

Setting Up Alerts for Failed iFlows in SAP Cloud Integration 

Monitoring integration failures is critical for reliable operations, and to support on automated alerts for failed iFlows in SAP Cloud Integration using SAP Alert Notification Service for SAP BTP, SAP Community member  Nikhil_Gursal provides a practical step-by-step guide showing how to create the Alert Notification service instance in SAP Business Technology Platform. He shows how to configure notification channels such as email, define event-based subscriptions for integration flow failures, and set up the standard alert notification iFlow that retrieves failed message logs and forwards them to the alert service. With screenshots covering configuration, service keys, OAuth credential setup, and testing scenarios, this blog post offers a clear and simple tips for integration developers who want to ensure they receive timely notifications whenever an iFlow fails. 

Read Nikhil blog post here 

Understanding Agentic Memory in AI Systems 

Agentic memory is becoming an important concept in modern AI systems and agent frameworks, enabling intelligent agents to "remember" past interactions and use that knowledge to improve future decisions. In this blog post, the SAP expert Lennart Adenaw explores what Agentic Memory actually means, when it should be used, and how it differs from traditional approaches like databases or retrieval systems. Using a first-principles perspective, the article explains which types of information are suitable for agentic memory, such as emergent insights, personalization signals, or lessons learned from feedback, and which are better handled through APIs, databases, or retrieval methods. The post also outlines key business use cases including personalization, contextual collaboration across sessions, multi-agent coordination, and agent self-improvement, while proposing a practical definition of agentic memory as a lifecycle system that captures, evolves, and retrieves knowledge derived from interactions. For everyone exploring AI agents and intelligent automation, this is a thoughtful introduction to how memory can enable more adaptive and effective agent behavior. 

Read Lennart blog post here 

Community News 

SAP’s Most Popular Certification Gets an Upgrade 

SAP’s popular certification for back-end developers, the Back-End Developer ABAP Cloud exam, is getting a huge upgrade! March also marks the SAP Community developer challenge for ABAP start, making this the perfect moment to sharpen your skills and validate your expertise. SAP expert Arabella, recently published a blog post on how the updated certification for ABAP developers moves beyond traditional multiple-choice exams to a hands-on, open-book format where candidates complete time-boxed tasks directly in live SAP systems. Built around realistic scenarios and AI-supported simulations, the exam allows access to learning resources such as the SAP Help Portal, SAP Community, and tools like Joule while emphasizing practical skills rather than memorization. She also highlights recommended learning paths, live learning sessions, and hands-on opportunities like SAP CodeJam events to help developers prepare. With the certification experience now aligned more closely with real project work, this is the perfect moment for community members to take advantage of the new format and not miss the chance to get certified and showcase their ABAP Cloud expertise. Make sure to check the entire content to find more valuable links to support you on your learning journey as well.  

Read Arabella blog post here 

New Location Groups! 

The end of February and beginning of March marked new milestones for our Location Groups! Now, if you are located in Chisinau, Linz or Mexico, you can join your local community group inside SAP Community and connect with members in your area. This marks a great outcome in our goal to keep growing the SAP community all over the world and bringing light to the great experts and learners in different countries. Through the location groups, you are able to share knowledge in your preferred language, build connections, and plan on-site meetups. And to keep everything moving, we would like to highlight our amazing new group curators Edgar, Maria Antonieta e Sandra (Mexico), Marcus (Linz), Natalia and Tsovinar (Chisinau)! 

Get to Know our SAP Mentors 

The SAP Mentors is a program that has been growing and bringing opportunities for SAP experts since 2007. And we are happy to acknowledge how much the program has grown and created valuable changes on behalf of our SAP ecosystem. Today, we would like to bring light to one of our oldest members, Gregor Wolf, SAP Mentor since 2007. Gregor is an independent SAP solution architect and developer, focused on SAP Business Technology Platform, SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP), Node.JS, SAP Fiori Elements and Security. He has supported several customers during their SAP Cloud ERP Private Edition (S/4HANA On Premise) implementation. Since the end of 2024, he is currently also supporting technical topics at an SAP Cloud ERP Public Edition Customer. SAP Advocate Stephanie Marley published a recent blog post, sharing details on why Gregor decided to become an SAP Mentor and what inspires him to be part of the community. Make sure to read it and give Gregor your support in the comments. We are more than grateful for having him and many others bringing expertise, sharing knowledge, and putting so much effort into our community!  

Read Stephanie blog post here 

Events 

UI5con Bengaluru 2026 Recap 

After a seven-year pause, UI5con returned to India with UI5con Bengaluru 2026, bringing together more than 400 developers, architects, consultants, and students for a full day of learning and community collaboration at SAP Labs. Freddy Jetty Johnson published a great recap blog post, highlighting key moments like the opening keynote exploring agentic coding and AI-assisted development with SAPUI5, to deep dives into modern UX development with SAP Fiori and new capabilities such as UI5 Web Components 2.0. With live demos, developer labs, and an interactive “Ask the Expert” panel featuring core UI5 experts, the event highlighted how AI, developer tooling, and community collaboration are shaping the future of UI development. 

SAP Inside Track Madrid 

We are happy to announce the SAP Inside Track Madrid 2026 on April 17, 2026, bringing together community members for a day of knowledge sharing, networking, and SAP discussions at the NEORIS Artificial Intelligence Hub. This community-driven event is the perfect place to learn from fellow experts, exchange ideas, and connect with other SAP professionals in an informal and collaborative setting. With space limited to just 70 attendees, spots are expected to fill quickly, so make sure to RSVP early to secure your place in the event page at SAP Community here. Also, if you would like to share your expertise, speaker proposals are also welcome, making this a wonderful opportunity to contribute to the community. Submit your proposal here.  

The SAP Intelligence Event in Amsterdam 

This week is happening the SAP Intelligence Event in Amsterdam, and many of our SAP Community Advocates are taking part to share more about their expertise and connect with local developers and SAP community members. This whole week (until March 11), the event is creating the perfect opportunity to understand more about AI Experience for SAP Developers, and we are also happy to have some of our SAP Mentors as speakers: Marla Schweiki, Robin Van Het Hof and Wouter Lemaire. In case you are participating, don´t forget to share more about your experience in our Community Corner! 

SAPinsider Las Vegas 2026 

Next week another great SAP event is going to take place in Las Vegas, Nevada, the SAPinsider 2026! From March 16 to 19, more than 2.000 SAP professionals are gathering to learn from 200+ experts across sessions, to understand what it is working and what needs to be improved. This is also a good opportunity to connect with local experts, get a better view on real-world strategies, customer case studies, and connect with SAP practitioners, architects, and business leaders. The Head of our SAP Community, Josh “Bluebeard” Bentley, will be present at the event and will bring a great recap for our Community Voice in the next few weeks!

entry.title: Вебінар: “SAP для України: відповідність рішень SAP вимогам українського законодавства та обліку
entry.content:
Вебінар: “SAP для України: відповідність рішень SAP вимогам українського законодавства та обліку”

📅 19 березня 2026

🕙 10:00–12:00

📍 Онлайн (Microsoft Teams)

Сьогодні багато українських компаній розглядають перехід із застарілих або небезпечних рішень на сучасні міжнародні системи. Проте існує поширений міф: «західне ПЗ не відповідає українському законодавству та бізнес‑процесам».

Ми хочемо чесно й прозоро показати, що насправді підтримує SAP S/4HANA у стандартній функціональності, а які компоненти реалізуються через проєктну кастомізацію чи разом із партнерами.

Запрошуємо вас на перший вебінар серії, присвячений локалізації SAP в Україні — для фінансових директорів, ІТ‑керівників, керівників трансформацій та всіх, хто шукає сучасну та безпечну ERP-систему.

Для кого цей вебінар?

  • компаній, що планують перехід на сучасну, безпечну та законодавчо відповідну ERP;
  • потенційних клієнтів, які вперше знайомляться з SAP;
  • організацій, що прагнуть замінити застарілі рішення та мінімізувати юридичні ризики;
  • фінансових, ІТ та операційних директорів.

Чому варто долучитися?

✔ прозоре пояснення: що входить у стандарт SAP, а що — у проєктну частину;
✔ зменшення міфів та страхів щодо законодавчих змін, швидкості впровадження та електронного документообігу;
✔ можливість поставити запитання напряму експертам SAP;
✔ участь у формуванні подальших тем для виствітлення.

Реєструйтеся за посиланням.

entry.title: Somos SAP Community Curitiba – Adriano Marques
entry.content:
Somos SAP Community Curitiba – Adriano Marques

Esta publicação faz parte da série Somos SAP Community Curitiba.

A ideia da série foi apresentada neste post de abertura:
Introdução - Série da comunidade: Somos SAP Community Curitiba

Fui convidado por @SimoneAzevedo para participar da série e compartilhar um pouco da minha jornada no ecossistema SAP.
Você pode conferir o post dela aqui:
Somos SAP Community Curitiba – Simone Azevedo - SAP Community

👨‍💻Quem sou eu na SAP Community Curitiba?

Sou Adriano Marques, empresário e curador da SAP Community de Curitiba.  Meu papel é contribuir  no fortalecimento e conexão dos profissionais SAP na região.

💙O que mais me motiva a continuar fazendo parte da SAP Community Curitiba?

O que mais me motiva são as pessoas e a troca genuína de conhecimento.  Projetos SAP costumam ser desafiadores, complexos e cheios de aprendizado. A comunidade cria um espaço onde profissionais podem compartilhar experiências reais — sucessos, desafios e lições aprendidas. 

Cada encontro da comunidade mostra algo muito claro:  quando profissionais compartilham conhecimento, todos evoluem.

🚀Como fazer parte da comunidade impactou minha jornada profissional ou pessoal?

Na conexão com profissionais com experiências e especialidades diferentes — consultores, arquitetos, gestores e especialistas — criou um ambiente extremamente rico de aprendizado para mim. O networking agregou na minha jornada.

Mas talvez o maior impacto seja outro: A comunidade cria oportunidades verdadeiras entre pessoas que acreditam em colaboração, aprendizado contínuo e evolução profissional.

❓Que conselho você daria para quem está começando agora no ecossistema SAP e ainda não conhece a comunidade?

Venha participar da nossa comunidade. Não perca tempo. Seu networking irá crescer imensamente, principalmente no início de sua carreira. Boas coisas acontecem quando estamos juntos.  

🌎Conecte-se comigo

LinkedIn 👉 https://www.linkedin.com/in/adrianomoliveira

✨Convidados para continuar a série

Para continuar a série Somos SAP Community Curitiba, convido:

 @Natacha_Wistuba @david_vilela88 @MarcosFerreira @davidson_alves_ti @Tiago_Fakeiti1 @beatrizsakai 

a compartilharem suas histórias na comunidade.

Além das perguntas da série:

1️⃣Quem sou eu na SAP Community Curitiba?
2️⃣O que mais me motiva a continuar fazendo parte da SAP Community Curitiba?
3️⃣Como fazer parte da comunidade impactou minha jornada profissional ou pessoal?

E deixo também uma pergunta adicional:

❓Para você, quais são as vantagens de participar da comunidade SAP ? Cite pelos menos 3.

✨Frase final

Tecnologia conecta sistemas.Comunidades conectam pessoas.  Cada história compartilhada fortalece ainda mais essa comunidade.

#SomosSAPCommunityCWB
#SAPCommunityCWB
#SAPCommunity

entry.title: SAP SuccessFactors Integration with Qualtrics - Part 2: Automating the New Hire Survey
entry.content:
In this blog, we'll configure an event-driven integration that automatically triggers a New Hire Survey when a new employee joins the organization. No manual participant uploads, Just a seamless, automated experience.

What You'll Learn

  • How to set up an event-based trigger using the Intelligent Services Center (ISC)
  • How to configure the Manage Qualtrics Integrations screen for email surveys
  • How the integration connects to the Lifecycle Project in Qualtrics
  • Why the Global Directory from Part 1 is essential for this to work

Prerequisites

Before you begin, make sure the following are in place:

 

#PrerequisiteDetails
1Part 1 CompletedEmployee directory sync is active and employees are flowing into the Qualtrics EX Global Directory
2Qualtrics Lifecycle ProjectThe Qualtrics consultant has created the New Hire Onboarding Survey as a Lifecycle Project and shared the Project ID
3Register Qualtrics in SuccessFactorsQualtrics authentication must be enabled in the Integration Service Registration Center. This is a one-time setup that allows SuccessFactors to communicate with Qualtrics.
4OAuth ConfigurationQualtrics API credentials (OAuth client) are configured in SuccessFactors
5Admin PermissionsAccess to Intelligent Services Center, Manage Qualtrics Integrations, and Integration Center in SuccessFactors

Architecture Overview

The flow works like this:

  1. A new hire is created in Employee Central
  2. The Intelligent Services Center/ISC detects the new hire event and waits 7 days after the start date
  3. After 7 days, the Manage Qualtrics Integrations email survey integration fires
  4. The integration sends the employee identifier to Qualtrics via API
  5. Qualtrics looks up the employee in the Global Directory (synced in Part 1)
  6. If found, the employee is added as a participant to the Lifecycle Project
  7. The survey invitation is sent automatically.

Step-by-Step Configuration

Step 1: Enable Qualtrics Authentication in Integration Service Registration Center

This is a one-time setup that allows SuccessFactors to communicate with the Qualtrics platform. If this has already been done for your instance, you can skip this step.

Navigation: Admin Center → Integration Service Registration Center

Step 2: Verify the Lifecycle Project in Qualtrics

Before configuring anything in SuccessFactors, confirm with your Qualtrics consultant that:

  • The Lifecycle Project (New Hire Onboarding Survey) has been created
  • The Project ID has been shared with you (e.g., TS_XXXXXXXXXX)
  • The survey is designed, tested, and ready to receive participants
  • The invitation email template and reminder settings are configured within the project

Note: The survey design, email templates, reminder schedule, and dashboard are all configured by the Qualtrics consultant within the Qualtrics platform. This blog focuses on the SAP SuccessFactors side of the integration.

Step 3: Create a Business Rule and Configure the Intelligent Services Center (ISC)

The Intelligent Services Center is where you define what event should trigger the integration and when it should fire. But first, you need to create a business rule that captures the new hire event.

Step 3a: Create the Business Rule

Before configuring the ISC event, you need to create a business rule that identifies when a new hire event occurs in Employee Central.

Navigation: Admin Center → Configure Business Rules

  1. Create a new business rule
  2. Define the rule to capture the hire event from Employee Central
  3. This rule acts as the filter - it tells the ISC exactly which employee events should trigger the survey integration

SAP Help Reference: For detailed steps on creating the business rule for email surveys, refer to the official SAP documentation: Creating Business Rule for Email Surveys

Tip: The business rule defines the "what" (which events to capture), while the ISC event subscription defines the "when" (timing of the trigger). Both work together to ensure the right employees receive the survey at the right time.

Step 3b: Configure the ISC Event Subscription

Once the business rule is created, configure the event subscription in the Intelligent Services Center.

Navigation: Admin Center → Intelligent Services Center

  1. Create a new event subscription
  2. Configure the following:

Setting Value

CategoryEmployee Central: Employee Recruitment
SourceIntegration Center
Timing7 Days After Effective Date
Business Rule(The business rule created in Step 3a)

       3. Save the event rule

 

What this does: The ISC monitors Employee Central for new hire events. When a new hire record is created and matches the business rule, the ISC doesn't fire immediately - it waits 7 days after the employee's effective start date before triggering the integration. This delay is configurable based on your organization's needs.

Step 4: Configure the Email Survey in Manage Qualtrics Integrations

This is where you create and configure the email survey integration that links the ISC event to the Qualtrics Lifecycle Project.

Navigation: Admin Center → Manage Qualtrics Integrations → Email Surveys

Click + Create New Integration and fill in the following:

#FieldValue
1Integration NameSF_NewHire
2Product AreaEmployee Central
3Event NameEmployee Recruitment
4Survey RecipientEmployee
5Business Scenario Name[Employee Central] Qualtrics: New Hire
6OAuth Configuration(Configured in Step 4b)
7Rule Configuration(Business rule from Step 3a)
8Survey Info - Project ID(Lifecycle Project ID from Qualtrics)

Fields 1–4 are self-explanatory. Let's look at the ones that need more detail:

Step 4a: Business Scenario Name

FieldValue
Business Scenario Name[Employee Central] Qualtrics: New Hire

This links the integration to the pre-delivered Integration Center business scenario that handles the data transfer to Qualtrics.

This is a standard pre-delivered business scenario. You don't need to create it - it becomes available automatically when the Qualtrics integration is enabled in your SuccessFactors instance.

Step 4b: OAuth Configuration

FieldValue
OAuth Configuration(Select your Qualtrics OAuth client)

The OAuth configuration provides the secure connection between SuccessFactors and your Qualtrics instance. This must be set up before you can activate the integration.

If the OAuth configuration has not been created yet:

SAP Help Reference: Follow the official SAP documentation for step-by-step instructions: Creating Outbound OAuth Configurations for Qualtrics APIs for Email Survey Integrations

Once created, return to the Edit Integration screen and select the OAuth configuration from the dropdown.

Step 4c: Rule Configuration

FieldValue
Rule Configuration(Select the business rule created in Step 3a)

This links the business rule you created in Step 2a. The rule acts as a filter - only events that match the rule (i.e., new hire events) will trigger the survey.

Step 4d: Survey Info - Project ID

FieldValue
Survey Info - Project IDTS_XXXXXXXXXX

This tells the integration which Qualtrics survey project should receive the participant.

Where to find the Project ID:

MethodHow
URL BarOpen the Lifecycle Project in Qualtrics. The Project ID is in the URL: https://yourorg.qualtrics.com/xxxx/ProjectID=TS_XXXXXXXXXX

Important: Use the Lifecycle Project ID, not a regular survey ID. The integration is designed to work with Qualtrics Lifecycle or Employee Engagement projects.

Step 4e: Review and Activate

Step 5: Activate the business scenario

This links the integration to the pre-delivered business scenario.

FieldValue
Business Scenario Name[Employee Central] Qualtrics: New Hire

Note: This is a standard pre-delivered business scenarios. You don't need to create it - it's automatically available when Qualtrics integration is enabled.

Step 6: Understand What Happens Behind the Scenes

Once the integration is active, here's what happens every time a new hire is created:

Day 0

  • New hire record created in Employee Central

Day 0–1

  • Part 1 daily sync adds the employee to the Qualtrics EX Global Directory

Day 7

  • ISC event fires based on the business rule
  • Integration sends the employee identifier to Qualtrics
  • Qualtrics looks up the employee in the Global Directory
  • Employee is added as a participant to the Lifecycle Project
  • Survey invitation email is sent to the new hire

Key Dependency: The employee must exist in the Qualtrics Global Directory before the Day 7 trigger fires. This is why Part 1 (Employee Directory Sync) is a critical prerequisite. The 7-day delay provides a natural buffer - the daily sync ensures the employee is in the directory well before Day 7.

Step 7: Verify the Integration

After activation, test the integration with a test employee:

  • Create a test new hire in Employee Central (or use an existing test employee in a sandbox)

             Employee was hired on 2nd March.

  • Wait for Part 1 sync to confirm the employee appears in the Qualtrics Global Directory
  • Trigger the ISC event (in a test environment, you may be able to trigger it manually without waiting 7 days)

  • Check the Lifecycle Project in Qualtrics to confirm the employee appears as a participant

  • Check the email to confirm the survey invitation was received
CheckpointWhat to verify
Global DirectoryEmployee appears with correct attributes
Lifecycle ProjectEmployee listed as a participant
Email InboxSurvey invitation received
Survey LinkSurvey opens correctly with personalized content
  • If the employee doesn't exist in the global directory, the sync will fail with below error.

What Does the Qualtrics Consultant Configure?

While this blog covers the SAP SuccessFactors side, it's helpful to understand what happens on the Qualtrics side. The Qualtrics consultant is responsible for:

Survey DesignQuestions, logic, branding
Invitation EmailEmail template with personalized fields (name, department, etc.) pulled from the Global Directory
RemindersAutomatic follow-up emails if the employee hasn't completed the survey (e.g., after 3 days, 7 days)
Survey ExpirationHow long the survey stays open
DashboardReporting and analytics for HR leaders
AnonymityConfidentiality and access control settings

Reminders are configured entirely within the Qualtrics Lifecycle Project. Once a participant is added by our integration, Qualtrics manages the full survey lifecycle - invitation, reminders, expiration - automatically.

Conclusion

This integration between SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central and Qualtrics automates the onboarding survey process — ensuring every new hire receives timely feedback requests without manual intervention.

Key Takeaways

  • Automation - Eliminates manual survey distribution using ISC rules and Integration Center
  • Timeliness - Captures new hire sentiment at the right moment
  • Accuracy - Employee data flows directly from EC to Qualtrics with no manual entry
  • Scalability - Works seamlessly irrespective of hire counts
  • Error Handling - Always account for edge cases like missing participants in Qualtrics

By combining the power of SAP SuccessFactors Integration Center, Intelligent Services, and Qualtrics, organizations can build a seamless, data-driven onboarding experience - capturing valuable employee feedback from Day 1.

Thanks for taking time to read..!

Coming Up Next

Blog 3: SAP SuccessFactors Integration with Qualtrics - Part 3: LMS Learning/Training Feedback

We'll explore how to trigger training feedback surveys automatically when an employee completes a learning course in SuccessFactors Learning - capturing real-time insights to continuously improve your L&D programs.

Stay tuned!

entry.title: Hello SAP community! My name is Shlok Shigli and I'm excited to start my journey
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community Post – Shlok Shigli

Introduction

Hello SAP Community! 👋

My name is Shlok Shigli, and I am currently pursuing Mechanical Engineering at REVA University in Bangalore, India.

I’m excited to be part of the Hello SAP Community initiative. This opportunity allows beginners like me to explore the SAP ecosystem, learn from experienced professionals, and connect with people across the global SAP network.

As a student, I am passionate about learning new technologies and understanding how SAP solutions help businesses manage operations more efficiently. Through the SAP Community, I look forward to gaining insights from experts, discovering new ideas, and building meaningful professional connections.

I’m excited to share my learning journey and contribute to the SAP Community as I continue to grow.

Thank you, SAP Community, for this wonderful opportunity!

1. What is your professional life dream?

My professional dream is to build a successful career at the intersection of technology and engineering. I want to continuously learn new skills and work on innovative solutions that help organizations improve efficiency and make better decisions. I also hope to contribute to the tech community and grow as a professional in the global technology ecosystem.

2. Where do you see yourself in 5 years?

In the next five years, I see myself as a skilled technology professional with strong technical knowledge and hands-on experience. I hope to work on real-world projects, continuously improve my expertise, and contribute positively to my organization and the wider technology community.

3. What is your hobby?

One of my hobbies is exploring new technologies and learning about innovations in engineering and business systems. I enjoy reading, learning new skills online, and continuously expanding my knowledge through different learning platforms.

4. Which 5 things do you absolutely want to achieve in life?

  • Build a strong career in the technology and engineering field
  • Continuously improve myself by learning new technologies and skills
  • Work on projects that create real-world impact
  • Build meaningful connections and collaborate with talented people
  • Maintain a balanced life while pursuing personal and professional growth

5. What is the best lesson your parents taught you?

One of the most important lessons my parents taught me is to stay disciplined and never stop learning. They always reminded me that progress takes time and consistency. Their guidance has helped me stay focused on my goals and approach challenges with patience and determination.

My Experience at SAP Inside Track Bengaluru

SAP Inside Track Bengaluru 2026 – Excited to be part of this amazing SAP Community eventSAP campus in Bengaluru

Recently, I had the opportunity to attend SAP Inside Track Bengaluru, and it was an incredibly valuable learning experience for me as a student exploring the SAP ecosystem.

Being part of an event where SAP professionals and community members shared their knowledge was very inspiring. It helped me better understand how SAP technologies are used to solve real-world business problems and improve organizational efficiency.

One of the most interesting parts of the event was interacting with SAP experts and fellow learners. Listening to their experiences and insights gave me a broader perspective on the different opportunities within the SAP ecosystem.

An insightful session on Generative AI and AI Agents,

Key Takeaways from the Event

  • The growing importance of AI and intelligent technologies in enterprise solutions
  • How SAP platforms help organizations improve efficiency and business processes
  • The strong role of the SAP Community in sharing knowledge and supporting learners.

I’m excited to continue learning and sharing my journey with the SAP Community as I explore more about SAP technologies

entry.title: Hello SAP Community! My name is Dino C D and I'm a Cybersecurity Graduate with a Passion for IoT, AI
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community!

My name is Dino C D and I'm a postgraduate student pursuing my MSc in Artificial Intelligence & Cybersecurity at Christ University, BYC, Bengaluru. I completed my Bachelor of Computer Applications at St Joseph's University, Bengaluru, where I built a strong foundation in programming, cloud deployment, full-stack development, and IoT engineering.

 

Beyond academics, I'm a multi-award-winning innovator (3× 1st Prize for my IoT IV Blood Detector Device), a patent applicant, an active cybersecurity community contributor through S.H.I.E.L.D, and a researcher working on audio deepfake detection. I love building things that solve real problems — whether that's a smart medical device, an interactive history website, or a security-aware web application.

Pitching my patent-pending IoT IV Blood Detector to a C-DAC judge

 

What is your professional life dream?

My professional dream is to become a leading cybersecurity engineer who doesn't just defend systems — but actively shapes the way security is built into technology from the ground up. I want to work at the intersection of AI and cybersecurity, developing intelligent threat detection systems that can anticipate attacks before they happen.

On a bigger scale, I want to contribute to making healthcare technology safer. My IoT IV Blood Detector project opened my eyes to how vulnerable critical medical infrastructure can be. My dream is to see a world where hospital systems, patient data, and medical IoT devices are protected by design — and I want to be part of building that future. A patent in progress feels like the first small step toward that dream.

 

 

 

 

Where do you see yourself in 5 years?

In five years, I see myself working as a SOC Analyst or Cybersecurity Researcher at a technology or cybersecurity firm — ideally contributing to threat intelligence, incident response, or AI-powered security tooling. I hope to have completed my MSc, earned certifications like CompTIA Security+ and CEH, and published at least one peer-reviewed research paper (starting with my current work on audio deepfake detection).

I also see myself mentoring the next wave of cybersecurity students — much like the mentors and communities that shaped me. If the journey goes the way I hope, perhaps I'll be sharing a very different blog post on the SAP Community in 2031, looking back at how it all started. 😊

 

Why did you choose MSc Artificial Intelligence & Cybersecurity at Christ University?

Honestly, it felt like the perfect convergence of everything I was already passionate about. After my BCA, I knew I wanted to go deeper — not just into cybersecurity, but into how AI is reshaping the entire threat landscape. Christ University's programme offered exactly that combination.

What truly won me over was the learning environment. The workshops have been eye-opening — hands-on, practical, and constantly pushing you to think beyond textbooks. The classes don't just teach concepts, they challenge you to question, build, and apply. Every session feels like it's preparing you for a problem that actually exists in the real world.

I'm genuinely grateful for the exposure this course has given me — from AI-driven security techniques to research opportunities. It's not just a degree; it's the foundation I needed to become the cybersecurity professional I want to be. 🎓


What was the most fun project you ever participated in and why?

Without a doubt: Temporal Explorer — Past to Future.

The idea was simple but ambitious — build an interactive website that lets users travel through time, from ancient civilisations all the way to speculative futures. We used HTML, CSS, and JavaScript to create animated timelines, artefact showcases, and immersive storytelling experiences.

What made it so fun wasn't just the tech — it was the creativity involved. We debated what the future might look like, researched historical artefacts, and designed transitions that felt like you were genuinely moving through eras. Completing it in time for the Web Weavers intercollegiate competition and walking away with 2nd place made it even more rewarding. It reminded me that the best projects aren't just technically sound — they tell a story.


What is your hobby?

My biggest hobby is community work and volunteering. Over the past few years, I've coordinated 50+ outreach events with AICUF, taught constitutional literacy in government schools through KJES, and helped build the cybersecurity awareness culture at Christ University through S.H.I.E.L.D.

It might sound unusual as a "hobby," but there's something deeply energising about connecting with people, solving real-world problems together, and seeing the direct impact of your effort — whether that's a student who finally understands their civic rights, or a community that feels a little more supported. It keeps me grounded in why I'm pursuing technology in the first place: to serve people.

A volunteering Social work which was held in Biccodu, Karnataka by KJES

 


What do you imagine or predict is the future of your topic — AI & Cybersecurity?

The future is fascinating — and a little terrifying, honestly. Here's my prediction:

AI will become the most powerful weapon in both attack and defence simultaneously. We're already seeing AI-generated phishing emails that are indistinguishable from real ones, deepfake audio used in fraud (which is exactly why I'm researching it), and autonomous malware that adapts in real time. The adversarial AI race is already here.

On the defence side, I believe AI-powered SOC platforms will eventually handle 90% of threat triage autonomously — flagging only the most complex incidents for human analysts. Security will shift from "detect and respond" to "predict and prevent."

The wildcard? IoT security. Billions of connected devices — many with zero security baked in — will be the biggest attack surface of the next decade. My IV detector project taught me first-hand how exposed medical IoT can be. I believe the next generation of cybersecurity professionals will spend as much time securing physical devices as they do securing data.

The future belongs to those who understand both the intelligence of AI and the vulnerabilities it creates.


Share a fun fact about yourself that people don't know!

I once helped coordinate a community event managing ₹2.5 lakh in organisational expenditure — and I was a second-year undergrad student at the time. 

As the core committee member for CHRONICLES (AICUF's major fest at St Joseph's University), I was suddenly responsible for budgeting, resource allocation, vendor coordination, and keeping everything on track. No one tells you that community leadership is basically a crash course in project management, crisis handling, and people skills — all at once.

Oh, and I did all of that while simultaneously building AWS deployments, learning Kali Linux, and developing IoT devices in my spare time. Sleep was optional, apparently.

With Justice Kurian Joseph, Retired Judge of the Supreme Court of India — Chief Guest at CHRONICLES, St Joseph's University, Bengaluru. ⚖️

 

And now let's hear from you!

What do you imagine or predict is the future of AI and Cybersecurity — and how are you preparing for it? Drop your thoughts in the comments below — I'd love to hear where you think the field is heading! 🚀

Dino C D | MSc AI & Cybersecurity Student, Christ University, Bengaluru
🔗linkedin.com/in/dinocd | 💻 github.com/Dino20004
